note_data.title: Introduction to Coordinate Geometry
keyPoints: Coordinate geometry is a branch of mathematics which deals with the study of geometry using coordinates
The coordinate system consists of two perpendicular lines called axes - horizontal axis (x-axis) and vertical axis (y-axis)
The point of intersection of x-axis and y-axis is called the origin, denoted by O(0,0)
Any point in the plane is represented by an ordered pair (x,y) where x is abscissa and y is ordinate
The coordinate axes divide the plane into four parts called quadrants - I, II, III, and IV
Distance between two points A(x₁,y₁) and B(x₂,y₂) is given by d = √[(x₂-x₁)² + (y₂-y₁)²]
Mid-point of line segment joining points A(x₁,y₁) and B(x₂,y₂) is ((x₁+x₂)/2, (y₁+y₂)/2)
Section formula divides a line segment internally in the ratio m:n

definitions.definition: Coordinate geometry is a branch of mathematics which deals with the study of geometry using coordinates.
The x-coordinate of a point is called its abscissa.
The y-coordinate of a point is called its ordinate.
The point of intersection of coordinate axes is called the origin.
The coordinate axes divide the plane into four parts called quadrants.

importantQuestions.question: Define coordinate geometry and write the coordinates of origin.
importantQuestions.answer: Coordinate geometry is a branch of mathematics which deals with the study of geometry using coordinates. The coordinates of origin are (0,0).

importantQuestions.question: Find the distance between points A(3,4) and B(7,1). Also find the coordinates of mid-point of AB.
importantQuestions.answer:
Distance formula: d = √[(x₂-x₁)² + (y₂-y₁)²]
d = √[(7-3)² + (1-4)²] = √[16 + 9] = √25 = 5 units
Mid-point formula: ((x₁+x₂)/2, (y₁+y₂)/2)
Mid-point = ((3+7)/2, (4+1)/2) = (5, 2.5)

mcqs.question: The point (-3, 2) lies in which quadrant?
The distance between points (0,0) and (3,4) is:

commonMistakes: Students often confuse abscissa with ordinate - remember x comes first, then y
Always write the distance formula before solving distance problems
Students forget to take square root in distance formula
Wrong signs in quadrants - remember ASTC rule for signs
Not simplifying coordinates properly in mid-point calculations
examTips: This chapter usually has 1-2 MCQs and 1 short question in every BSEK paper
Memorize the quadrant diagram with signs - it appears every year
Distance and mid-point formulas are must-know for long questions
Practice plotting points on coordinate plane for better understanding
note_data.keyDiagram: Draw coordinate axes with origin O(0,0), mark all four quadrants (I, II, III, IV), show positive and negative directions on both axes, and indicate signs of coordinates in each quadrant: I(+,+), II(-,+), III(-,-), IV(+,-)
pastPaperRefs: BSEK 2023 SSC-II Q3 (4 marks) — asked students to define coordinate axes and quadrants with proper labeling
BSEK 2022 SSC-II Q2 (3 marks) — required plotting points and identifying their quadrants
BSEK 2021 SSC-II Q4 (5 marks) — tested coordinate plane setup and coordinate identification
BSEK 2020 SSC-II Q1 (3 marks) — asked for exact definitions of origin, abscissa, and ordinate

workedExample.question: Define coordinate axes and identify the coordinates of point P shown in the given figure where P is located 3 units right and 2 units up from origin. (5 marks)
steps: Define coordinate axes: Two perpendicular number lines intersecting at origin
X-axis is horizontal line, Y-axis is vertical line
Point P is 3 units right from origin along x-axis
Point P is 2 units up from origin along y-axis
workedExample.answer: Coordinate axes are two perpendicular number lines that intersect at a point called origin. The horizontal line is called x-axis and vertical line is called y-axis. The coordinates of point P are (3, 2) where 3 is the abscissa and 2 is the ordinate.
workedExample.markScheme: 2 marks for correct definition of coordinate axes, 1 mark for identifying x-axis and y-axis, 2 marks for correct coordinates (3, 2)

examinerWarnings: Students often lose marks by writing 'x-coordinate and y-coordinate' instead of textbook terms 'abscissa and ordinate'. Always use STBB textbook terminology.
Many students forget to mention that coordinate axes are 'perpendicular' and intersect at 'origin'. Include all components of the textbook definition.
When identifying quadrants, students must mention the signs of coordinates (+ or -) for each quadrant as given in STBB textbook, not just the quadrant names.
note_data.quickRecap: Coordinate geometry uses two perpendicular number lines (coordinate axes) intersecting at origin to locate points. X-axis is horizontal, Y-axis is vertical. Every point has coordinates (x, y) where x is abscissa and y is ordinate. The plane is divided into four quadrants with different sign combinations.
